citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
It was found on records inspection that the facility has a recent history of not reviewing the research facility's program for
humane care and use of animals every 6 months. There were IACUC meetings held on March 30, 2024, November 5,
2024, and most recently June 24, 2025. There has not been an IACUC meeting in over 6 months for the last 3
consecutive meetings. There was only one meeting in 2025, and so the next one is also overdue.
It is important to correct any forward drift of meetings and to hold them as often as necessary to adequately review and
conduct oversight of the facility's animal care and use program.
Review, at least once every six months, the research facility's program for humane care and use of animals, using title 9,
chapter I, subchapter A--Animal Welfare, as a basis for evaluation.
Correct by February 28, 2026, and forward.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
The facility has a recent history of not conducting semiannual facility inspections at least once every 6 months. The
inspections were conducted on March 30, 2024, November 5, 2024, and most recently on June 24, 2025. There has not
been a semiannual facility inspection in over 6 months for the last 3 consecutive inspections. There was only one
inspection in 2025, and so the next one is also overdue.
Failure to conduct semiannual inspections of the animal facilities at least once every six months may hinder Institutional
Official’s awareness of challenges and problems present. This could delay the necessary corrective actions to restore
animal facilities and animal study areas that are deficient.
Inspect, at least once every six months, all of the research facility's animal facilities, including animal study areas, using
title 9, chapter I, subchapter A-Animal Welfare, as a basis for evaluation.
Correct by February 28, 2026, and forward.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
On records inspection, the November 2024 Semiannual Report to the Institutional Official, IO, (after the November 5,
2024, IACUC meeting and semiannual inspection), was not available or presented after being requested at the time of the
inspection. In addition, the July 31, 2025, Semiannual Report to the IO was missing the required documentation that it had
been reviewed and signed by a majority of the IACUC members.
Failure to properly submit Semiannual Reports to the IO at least once every six months may hinder the IO's awareness of
the facility's challenges, departures and issues relating to the care and use of animals at the facility. This could result in
the delay of necessary corrective actions to restore deficiencies found in animal facilities and animal study areas. The
responsibilities and actions of the IACUC must be demonstrated to provide and maintain adequate oversight of animal
care and use at the facility.
Prepare reports of its evaluations conducted as required by paragraphs (c)(1) and (2) of this section, and submit the
reports to the Institutional Official of the research facility; Provided, however, That the IACUC may determine the best
means of conducting evaluations of the research facility's programs and facilities; and Provided, further, That no
Committee member wishing to participate in any evaluation conducted under this subpart may be excluded. The IACUC
may use subcommittees composed of at least two Committee members and may invite ad hoc consultants to assist in
conducting the evaluations, however, the IACUC remains responsible for the evaluations and reports as required by the
Act and regulations. The reports shall be reviewed and signed by a majority of the IACUC members and must include any
minority views. The reports shall be updated at least once every six months upon completion of the required semiannual
evaluations and shall be maintained by the research facility and made available to APHIS and to officials of funding
Federal agencies for inspection and copying upon request. The reports must contain a description of the nature and
extent of the research facility's adherence to this subchapter, must identify specifically any departures from the provisions
of title 9, chapter I, subchapter A--Animal Welfare, and must state the reasons for each departure. The reports must
distinguish significant deficiencies from minor deficiencies. A significant deficiency is one which, with reference to
Subchapter A, and, in the judgment of the IACUC and the Institutional Official, is or may be a threat to the health or safety
of the animals. If program or facility deficiencies are noted, the reports must contain a reasonable and specific plan and
schedule with dates for correcting each deficiency. Any failure to adhere to the plan and schedule that results in a
significant deficiency remaining uncorrected shall be reported in writing within 15 business days by the IACUC, through
the Institutional Official, to APHIS and any Federal agency funding that activity.
Correct by March 31, 2026, and forward.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
On inspection, it was found that a Principal Investigator had submitted an amendment to a protocol that involved an
additional procedure involving a device implantation, anesthesia, and possibly additional analgesia. The application for
protocol amendment was submitted on 11/07/2025 and was approved by the Attending Veterinarian (AV) on 11/10/2025.
It appears that the AV approved the additional procedure and device without the approval of the IACUC, even though the
box on the approval form was checked, which states: "The changes proposed have received GMD IACUC review, per
guidelines for review procedures for significant changes in an approved application for vertebrate animal use." The
inspector was told that the last official IACUC meeting was on June 24, 2025, and there was no documentation provided
to show that the IACUC actually approved these significant changes or was made aware of them.
Approval by the IACUC is necessary for proposed activities and proposed significant changes in ongoing activities that
involve or affect the animals in the study. The IACUC should be aware of and have a role in the decision of proposed
significant changes for protocols that involve and affect the animals at the facility. The IACUC needs adequate knowledge
about and oversight for animal use and care at the facility.
In order to approve proposed activities or proposed significant changes in ongoing activities, the IACUC shall conduct a
review of those components of the activities related to the care and use of animals and determine that the proposed
activities are in accordance with this subchapter unless acceptable justification for a departure is presented in writing;
Provided, however, That field studies as defined in part 1 of this subchapter are exempt from this requirement. Further,
the IACUC shall determine that the proposed activities or significant changes in ongoing activities meet the following
requirements: see additional regs.
Correct by February 28, 2026, and forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(1) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
It was found on records inspection that the facility has a recent history of not reviewing the research facility's program for

2024, and most recently June 24, 2025. There has not been an IACUC meeting in over 6 months for the last 3 
consecutive meetings. There was only one meeting in 2025, and so the next one is also overdue. 
 
It is important to correct any forward drift of meetings and to hold them as often as necessary to adequately review and 
conduct oversight of the facility's animal care and use program. 
 
Review, at least once every six months, the research facility's program for humane care and use of animals, using title 9, 
chapter I, subchapter A--Animal Welfare, as a basis for evaluation.  
 
Correct by February 28, 2026, and forward. 
2.31(c)(2) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
The facility has a recent history of not conducting semiannual facility inspections at least once every 6 months. The

inspection in 2025, and so the next one is also overdue.  
 
Failure to conduct semiannual inspections of the animal facilities at least once every six months may hinder Institutional 
Official’s awareness of challenges and problems present. This could delay the necessary corrective actions to restore 
animal facilities and animal study areas that are deficient. 
 
Inspect, at least once every six months, all of the research facility's animal facilities, including animal study areas, using 
title 9, chapter I, subchapter A-Animal Welfare, as a basis for evaluation.

Correct by February 28, 2026, and forward. 
2.31(c)(3) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
On records inspection, the November 2024 Semiannual Report to the Institutional Official, IO, (after the November 5, 
2024, IACUC meeting and semiannual inspection), was not available or presented after being requested at the time of the 
inspection. In addition, the July 31, 2025, Semiannual Report to the IO was missing the required documentation that it had 
been reviewed and signed by a majority of the IACUC members.  
 
Failure to properly submit Semiannual Reports to the IO at least once every six months may hinder the IO's awareness of 
the facility's challenges, departures and issues relating to the care and use of animals at the facility. This could result in 
the delay of necessary corrective actions to restore deficiencies found in animal facilities and animal study areas. The 
responsibilities and actions of the IACUC must be demonstrated to provide and maintain adequate oversight of animal 
care and use at the facility. 
 
Prepare reports of its evaluations conducted as required by paragraphs (c)(1) and (2) of this section, and submit the

Committee member wishing to participate in any evaluation conducted under this subpart may be excluded. The IACUC

of the animals. If program or facility deficiencies are noted, the reports must contain a reasonable and specific plan and

significant deficiency remaining uncorrected shall be reported in writing within 15 business days by the IACUC, through 
the Institutional Official, to APHIS and any Federal agency funding that activity.

2.31(d)(1) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
On inspection, it was found that a Principal Investigator had submitted an amendment to a protocol that involved an 
additional procedure involving a device implantation, anesthesia, and possibly additional analgesia. The application for

protocol amendment was submitted on 11/07/2025 and was approved by the Attending Veterinarian (AV) on 11/10/2025. 
It appears that the AV approved the additional procedure and device without the approval of the IACUC, even though the

Approval by the IACUC is necessary for proposed activities and proposed significant changes in ongoing activities that

In order to approve proposed activities or proposed significant changes in ongoing activities, the IACUC shall conduct a 
review of those components of the activities related to the care and use of animals and determine that the proposed 
activities are in accordance with this subchapter unless acceptable justification for a departure is presented in writing; 
Provided, however, That field studies as defined in part 1 of this subchapter are exempt from this requirement. Further, 
the IACUC shall determine that the proposed activities or significant changes in ongoing activities meet the following 
requirements: see additional regs. 
 
Correct by February 28, 2026, and forward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
